vocab.meaning: dựa dẫm, cậy nhờ vào ai đó (hỗ trợ tinh thần/thể chất)
vocab.example: When you're having an off-day in football, you can lean on your teammates to pick up the slack.

vocab.meaning: cố gắng hết sức, khai phá sức mạnh nội tại
vocab.example: Individual athletes often have to dig deep to find the mental strength to finish a race alone.

vocab.meaning: phối hợp, cùng nhau nỗ lực làm việc
vocab.example: Success in basketball depends on how well the five players on the court pull together during the final minutes.

vocab.meaning: chấp nhận và đối mặt với (thực tế/thất bại)
vocab.example: In solo sports, you have to face up to your own technical flaws without anyone else to share the blame.

vocab.meaning: giúp ai đó vượt qua khó khăn
vocab.example: The collective energy of a team can carry a struggling player through a particularly grueling match.

vocab.meaning: tính đến, xem xét một yếu tố nào đó
vocab.example: Individual sports are unique because you don't have to factor in someone else's rhythm or playing style.
It is much easier to stand out as a prodigy in swimming than it is in a team where the spotlight is shared.

vocab.meaning: giải quyết, khắc phục (những vấn đề nhỏ/mâu thuẫn)
vocab.example: Team sports require a lot of time to iron out communication issues between players to ensure a smooth performance.

vocab.meaning: theo kịp, duy trì cùng tốc độ với người khác
vocab.example: In team-based cycling, you have to work twice as hard to keep up with the lead rider's pace.

vocab.meaning: chiến thắng, vượt qua (nghịch cảnh/đối thủ)
vocab.example: The feeling of a whole city triumphing over a rival team is vastly different from an individual’s personal victory.
